name: routing
summary: Classify input type and dispatch to the right specialized handler. Claude acts as the classifier + router.
when_to_use: When the task can be cleanly partitioned into 2+ input classes, each needing different expertise or model.

layers.CLAUDE.md: Add routing table: 'Auth tasks → {auth-dir} + security-compliance-advisor. DB tasks → {db-dir} + migration-lead. Frontend → ui-reviewer.'
layers.skill: skills/agentic-patterns documents the general shape; actual routing lives in CLAUDE.md.
layers.agent: Route may invoke a specialist agent (security-compliance-advisor, migration-lead, etc.)

cost: ~1× normal. Routing itself is cheap; specialized handlers may be cheaper than generalist (Haiku for retrieval) or more expensive (Opus for reasoning).
anti_patterns: Routing table with overlapping classes — ambiguity defeats the pattern.
Over-specializing: routing to 10 branches when 3 would cover 90% of cases.
